title: sterculia alexandri
intro: Sterculia alexandri, also known as the Alexandrian sterculia, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Sterculiaceae. The plant's roots, bark, and leaves are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sterculia alexandri include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a treatment for diarrhea and dysentery. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of decoctions or infusions, with the roots being more commonly used for medicinal purposes.
